steps.title: Starting list
steps.body: We will sort $[5, 2, 9, 1, 6]$ into ascending order using bubble sort. Each **pass** scans the list left to right, comparing every pair of neighbours and swapping them if the left value is bigger than the right value.

steps.title: Pass 1
steps.body:
Compare each adjacent pair in turn:

- $(5, 2)$: $5 > 2$, so **swap** → $[2, 5, 9, 1, 6]$
- $(5, 9)$: $5 < 9$, no swap
- $(9, 1)$: $9 > 1$, so **swap** → $[2, 5, 1, 9, 6]$
- $(9, 6)$: $9 > 6$, so **swap** → $[2, 5, 1, 6, 9]$

End of pass 1: $[2, 5, 1, 6, 9]$. The largest value, $9$, has "bubbled" all the way to its final position at the end.

steps.title: Pass 2
steps.body:
The last element ($9$) is already in its final place, so this pass only needs to compare the first four elements:

- $(2, 5)$: no swap
- $(5, 1)$: $5 > 1$, so **swap** → $[2, 1, 5, 6, 9]$
- $(5, 6)$: no swap

End of pass 2: $[2, 1, 5, 6, 9]$.

steps.title: Pass 3
steps.body:
The last two elements are now fixed, so this pass compares the first three elements:

- $(2, 1)$: $2 > 1$, so **swap** → $[1, 2, 5, 6, 9]$
- $(2, 5)$: no swap

End of pass 3: $[1, 2, 5, 6, 9]$.

steps.title: Pass 4 — no swaps needed
steps.body:
Comparing the remaining pair $(1, 2)$ makes no swap. Since a full pass completed with **zero swaps**, the list must already be sorted, and bubble sort can stop early: $[1, 2, 5, 6, 9]$.

In total this sort made $3 + 1 + 1 = 5$ swaps across its passes.
